resources.string: Location permission is required to get WiFi information
Are you sure you want to clear all history?
Optional. If set, appended as HTTP Authorization: Bearer header
Auto-query AP info on BSSID change

This app helps you understand the operation status of campus WiFi networks on Android. It retrieves BSS information of the connected AP by sending the BSS MAC address (stripped of -: separators) as the bssid=XXXXXXXXXXXX parameter to the configured query URL. If a query KEY is set, it is included as the HTTP Authorization: Bearer header. The query URL may optionally return {"status":"ok","data":[{"BSS_MAC":"19c97ad55e00","AP_NAME":"wlzx-13895-207","AP_SN":"219801A6M28257E00XXX","AP_MAC":"19c97ad55e00","AC_IP":"x.x.x.x","BAND":"2","SSID":"ssid","AP_Building":"Bldg A - Network Center","AP_IP":"x.x.x.x"}]}.

For self-hosted query API setup, refer to the api directory in the project source.

Local BSSMAC database: Supports editing and saving BSSMAC info on-device (Settings → BSSMAC Info). Queries use local data first, falling back to the remote API. Supports batch add, edit, delete, and export.

In the history view, tap a record to edit and save it locally.

On the signal chart, red dots indicate AP handover (roaming) events.
One per line: BSSMAC AP-Name [Building] (BSSMAC supports xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx or xxxx-xxxx-xxxx format; building is optional)
Saved to local BSS MAC database
Save failed: invalid BSS MAC format

This app needs the following permissions to read WiFi info:

%1$s

Tap OK, then grant in the system prompt.
• Location: Android 10+ requires location permission to access WiFi BSSID
• Nearby devices: Android 13+ requires this to scan nearby WiFi devices

v1.35 English UI support
- Created values-en/strings.xml with English translations
- Added English project introduction page (introduction_en.html)
- Refactored SignalLevel enum to use string resource IDs
- Migrated hardcoded Chinese strings in MainActivity.kt to strings.xml
- UI automatically switches to English when system language is set to English

v1.34 Bug fixes and local query improvements
- Fixed auto-query not updating AP names from local BSSMAC results

v1.33 Dark theme and UI optimization
- Dark theme support (color resources, MaterialAlertDialog)
- Locked portrait orientation
- Prioritize local database for BSSMAC name lookup
- Scrollable settings page
- Stats menu (AP switch, local query, cache, remote query)

v1.32 Display improvements and about page enhancements
- DataStore migration, deprecated SharedPreferences
- Moved hardcoded strings to strings.xml
- Show raw JSON in query results
- Added detailed intro button in About page

For full changelog, see Chinese version.

This app collects the following information at runtime:

The BSSID (MAC address) and signal strength (RSSI) of the currently connected and nearby APs sharing the same SSID, used for AP info queries and signal chart display.

The BSSID (MAC address) will be sent to the query URL configured in Settings. You can disable queries, modify, or clear the URL to prevent data transmission.

All history and BSSMAC data is stored locally on the device and is not transmitted to any other server.

Location permission is required by the Android system; this app does not collect or process location information.

This app does not collect or process any personally identifiable information (name, account, device, location, etc.).
